verse_text: Also the swine is unclean for you, because it has cloven hooves, yet does not chew the cud; you shall not eat their flesh or touch their dead carcasses.

links.context: Some teach that the presence of swine indicates a Gentile population. However, since our Lord forbade His disciples to go to the Gentiles ([[MAT.10:5]]) and was Himself reluctant to seek out the Gentiles ([[MAT.15:24]]), likely these were Jews engaged in a sinful occupation (see also the note at [[MAT.4:15]]). The destruction of the entire herd reveals: (1) while animals are a venerable part of creation, man is of far greater value; (2) Christ removes a source of sin, for swine herding was an abomination to the Jews ([[DEU.14:8]]); (3) demons have no power over creation, but are subject to the will of God--they could only enter the swine at Christ's command; (4) people are protected under God's providence--otherwise, the demon-possessed man would have come to the same end as the swine; (5) the magnitude of the economic loss would remain as a sign for the hardhearted people who fled that region (v. 34).

symbolic.title: Returning to the Garden of Eden

links.context: The bread of the Gentiles is described in [[DEU.14:3]]-21 as unclean food for the people of God.

symbolic.title: Returning to the Garden of Eden
